free_text: The giant dipole resonance built on excited states in
113,114Sn and level densities in the vicinity of the
neutron separation energy.
Data for the incident energy
of 24 MeV are given.
5.2 mg/cm2 110Cd target, enriched to 96%, was placed
at angle of  55 degrees with respect to beam axis.
110Cd target contained 1.6% 111Cd and 1.1% 112Cd
impurity. Contributions of these impurities to gamma
ray spectra were determined in two additional runs
using 4 mg/cm2 111Cd target and 5.2 mg/cm2 112Cd
target.
Prompt high-energy gamma-rays were detected
with large (diameter 40 cm, length 40 cm) NaI(Tl)
detector consisting of 6 identical, optically-separated
segments and a small-diameter(8 cm)detector, used as
single-photon spectrometer. Delayed gamma-rays were
detected with three NaI(Tl) detectors, two of which had
diameters of 28 cm and lengths of 28 cm and  third one
had diameter of 13 cm and length of 13 cm, placed as
close to target as possible(10 cm).
Ge detector was used for normalization of cross
section.
Absolute normalization of spectra was made using cross
section from 2+ to 0+ transition in 112 Sn.
Spectra were corrected for contribution of target
impurities.
Digitizing error of outgoing particle energy
Data digitizing error
